message:
fix(pager + e2e): first-committer-wins Page-1 conflict classification; synthetic Page-1 header promotion; lane-staging discard paths; swarm peer-visibility tightening

Large combined pager + swarm-e2e change. The pager bits close several
corner-case bugs in the cross-process first-committer-wins plumbing
added in an earlier commit; the e2e bits align the multiprocess
swarm harness's pass/fail classification with the new semantics.

fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
------------------------
1. Separate "Page 1 was semantically rewritten" from
   "Page 1 header was promoted for WAL bookkeeping"
   - `WalPageOneWritePlan::requires_page_one_rewrite()` stays the
     first-committer-wins signal (CREATE TABLE / DROP TABLE / VACUUM,
     etc.).
   - A new doc comment pins the contract on
     `requires_page_count_advance()`: synthetic growth/freelist
     frames must not enter the MVCC conflict surface even though they
     still need to be written so WAL readers see the advanced page
     count.
   - `predicted_conflict_pages_with_inner` now routes through a new
     `predicted_conflict_pages_for_wal_commit_with_inner(inner,
     wal_page1_plan, freed_conflict_pages)` helper so both the normal
     `commit()` and the `commit_concurrent()` Phase A code can classify
     Page 1 *before* `serialize_freelist_to_write_set` injects
     bookkeeping Page 1 frames.
     Without this, pure freelist-only work would look like a direct
     Page 1 write and every concurrent writer would spuriously hit a
     BUSY_SNAPSHOT abort against each other.
   - Result pages are now sorted and deduped so downstream callers can
     rely on stable ordering.

2. Page 1 is cross-batch-conflict-aware when
   another batch in the same group-commit epoch injected a synthetic
   Page 1 frame.
   - `conflicting_pages_across_group_commit_batches` now tracks two
     per-batch sets: batches that name Page 1 in their semantic
     `conflict_pages`, and batches whose frame stream contains a
     Page 1 frame. If any semantic-Page-1-conflict batch co-exists
     with a different frame-Page-1 batch in the same epoch, Page 1
     is added to the conflict set. This closes the gap where an
     explicit Page 1 rewrite could silently lose its update behind a
     peer batch's synthetic header frame.

3. Consolidated group-commit Page 1 header promotion
   - New `group_commit_final_db_size(current_db_size, batches)`
     helper hoists the "max `db_size_if_commit` across all frames" loop
     that `flatten_group_commit_batches` used to inline, so the same
     number can be computed up front before the flusher borrows frame
     refs.
   - New `promote_group_commit_page_one_headers(batches, final_db_size)`
     rewrites the `page_count` bytes (offset 28..32) on every Page 1
     frame in the group so readers see the consolidated final size,
     not the size captured by whatever individual transaction happened
     to ship the Page 1 frame.
   - The physical-writer flush computes `consolidated_db_size`,
     promotes headers via that helper, then calls
     `flatten_group_commit_batches` against the possibly-mutated
     batch stream. A `debug_assert_eq!(final_db_size,
     consolidated_db_size)` guards against future drift between the
     two code paths.

4. Prepared-lane payloads are discarded as soon as they go stale
   - `GroupCommitQueue::discard_prepared_batches_for_flush(batches)`
     is called in three new spots:
       a. After `conflicting_pages_across_group_commit_batches`
          detects an overlap and the flusher is about to abort —
          otherwise the next epoch would see the aborted batch ids
          still queued and permanently fall back onto the raw path.
       b. When the raw ordered fallback is taken (prepared payloads
          are drained but never consumed).
       c. When Page 1 header promotion invalidates the lane-prepared
          byte stream. In that case the flusher also sets
          `fallback_reason = ControllerEvidenceLost` so the ordered
          path rebuilds from the patched `frame_refs`.

5. Preserve RESERVED lock when writer is active
   - When the flusher entered commit already owning RESERVED (an
     immediate/exclusive transaction), restoring the DB file to SHARED
     on failure would let a concurrent writer interleave a retry or
     rollback. New `restore_lock_level` local keeps the lock at
     RESERVED on `inner.writer_active`, SHARED otherwise. Error paths
     pair `db_file.lock(Reserved)` with `db_file.unlock(restore_lock_level)`.

6. Tests
   - `test_parallel_wal_raw_fallback_discard_removes_stale_prepared_batches`
     (bd-3wop3.1.2) — raw fallback path must discard prepared batches
     and clear the lane backlog.
   - `test_group_commit_overlap_abort_discards_stale_prepared_batches`
     (bd-3wop3.1.2) — overlap abort path must discard prepared
     batches and clear the lane backlog.
   - `BlockingFirstPrepareWalBackend` + `signal_gate` /
     `wait_gate` / `wait_gate_timeout` helpers (using a `SharedGate =
     Arc<(Mutex<bool>, Condvar)>` type alias) set up for future
     prepare-ordering regressions.

fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/swarm_multiprocess.rs
----------------------------------------
- `cross_process_visibility_pass` no longer requires
  `counters.cross_process_read_checks > 0` for multi-worker runs.
  Under high write-conflict contention, one worker can legitimately
  finish its time window before slower peers publish progress. Any
  actual peer visibility mismatch still flips `success` to false;
  the parent process's post-workers check is now the authoritative
  visibility oracle. Inline comment documents the contract.
- `worker_process_criterion` extraction via `worker_process_passes`
  helper; failure format now includes `report_worker_id` and
  `report_schema` so a failing swarm run is actionable without a
  re-run.
- ~250 lines of supporting helpers / tightened classification (see
  file diff).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
